response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more than Emily which made his total 39 blueberries. Additionally David gathered three times as many blueberries as Sophia. This means Sophia collected 13 blueberries. Together Emily David and Sophia collected a total of 24 plus 39 plus 13 blueberries. That equals 76 blueberries in total.

***

They used all the blueberries to make muffins. Each batch of muffins required 9 blueberries. To find out how many batches they could make divide the total number of blueberries by the number needed per batch. 76 divided by 9 gives 8 full batches with some blueberries left over. They sold each batch for $5. Multiplying the number of batches by the price per batch gives the total amount of money they made.

***

The calculation for the money made is 8 times 5. This equals 40 dollars. The leftover blueberries were not enough to make another batch so they could not sell them. The total earnings from the muffins is 40 dollars. They used all the blueberries they collected to make the muffins and sold them at the market.

***

The total amount of money they made from their blueberry collection is 40 dollars. This was achieved by making 8 batches of muffins and selling each for $5. Their efforts resulted in a profitable outcome. The youth who collected and sold the blueberries benefited from their hard work.
